summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall exfiltrates developer/SCM/cloud identity to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and harvests developer identity material from the installer's home directory: hostname, username, git user.email from ~/.gitconfig and the project's.git/config, committer emails parsed from.git/logs/HEAD reflog, GitHub CLI identity from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, SSH public-key comment emails from ~/.ssh/*.pub, GCP project/account from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, DNS search domain from /etc/resolv.conf, parent project package.json metadata, CI provider, and cwd. The aggregated JSON payload is POSTed to a hardcoded endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. The package name and 'TypeScript utilities for the Anthropic SDK' description impersonate association with Anthropic to lure developers; no actual library code ships (the declared `main` dist/index.js is absent from the tarball). The 'anonymous diagnostics' framing is a cover story — the installer did not opt into having ~/.gitconfig, ~/.ssh/*.pub, ~/.aws/config, gcloud properties, and git reflog emails sent to a third party.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
